frameworks.name: multifractal analysis
frameworks.one_line: characterizes non-linear non-stationary non-Markovian spike interval dynamics

frameworks.name: spiking neural networks
frameworks.one_line: trained to generate goal-directed architectures for task differentiation

core_question: How does individual neuronal spiking behavior reflect the structure and function of the underlying network?
key_assumptions: interspike intervals carry non-Markovian temporal structure
network architecture shapes individual spiking dynamics
multifractal measures capture features beyond conventional spike statistics
key_findings: multifractal spike-interval profile is sensitive to network structure
multifractal profile is relatively consistent across stimuli
profile is not influenced by unobserved neuronal ensembles
trained SNNs with different tasks show distinguishable multifractal signatures
interpretations: multifractal spike analysis decodes underlying circuit connectivity from sparse spike data
individual spike dynamics provide a window onto goal-directed network architecture
